    Cache

    Cache.

    CacheBackend

    Bases: Protocol

    Protocol for cache storage backends.

    All methods are async because backends typically perform I/O. Backends are pure key-value stores: TTL, eviction, and statistics are managed by TTLCache.

    __aenter__ async 

    __aenter__() -> Self

    Open the backend connection.

    __aexit__ async 

    __aexit__(
    exc_type: type[BaseException] | None,
    exc_value: BaseException | None,
    traceback: TracebackType | None,
) -> None

    Close the backend connection.

    get async 

    get(*, key: str) -> bytes | None

    Get raw bytes by key.

    Returns None if the key is missing or expired.

    set async 

    set(*, key: str, value: bytes, ttl: float) -> None

    Store raw bytes with a TTL in seconds.

    delete async 

    delete(*, key: str) -> None

    Delete a key (no-op if absent).

    clear async 

    clear() -> None

    Remove all entries managed by this backend.

    CacheError

    Bases: GrelmicroError

    Base cache error.

    CacheInfo dataclass 

    CacheInfo(
    hits: int,
    misses: int,
    maxsize: int,
    currsize: int,
    evictions: int,
)

    Cache statistics snapshot.

    Attributes:

    Name Type Description
    hits int

    Number of cache hits.
    misses int

    Number of cache misses.
    maxsize int

    Maximum number of entries (0 means unlimited).
    currsize int

    Current number of tracked entries.
    evictions int

    Number of entries evicted to make room.

    CacheSettingsValidationError 

    CacheSettingsValidationError(error: ValidationError | str)

    Bases: CacheError, SettingsValidationError

    Cache Settings Validation Error.

    TTLCache 

    TTLCache(
    maxsize: Annotated[
        int,
        Doc(
            "\n                Maximum number of entries. ``0`` means unlimited.\n                Only enforced locally (not by the backend).\n                "
        ),
    ] = 0,
    ttl: Annotated[
        float,
        Doc(
            "\n                Default TTL in seconds for all entries.\n                "
        ),
    ] = 60,
    *,
    backend: Annotated[
        CacheBackend | None,
        Doc(
            "\n                The cache storage backend.\n\n                By default, the registered cache backend is used.\n                "
        ),
    ] = None,
    serializer: Annotated[
        Callable[[T], bytes] | None,
        Doc(
            "\n                Optional function to serialize values to bytes\n                before storing in the backend.\n                "
        ),
    ] = None,
    deserializer: Annotated[
        Callable[[bytes], T] | None,
        Doc(
            "\n                Optional function to deserialize bytes from the\n                backend back into values.\n                "
        ),
    ] = None,
)

    Bases: Generic[T]

    Cache with per-entry TTL and optional LRU eviction.

    Delegates storage to a CacheBackend (in-memory, Redis, etc.). TTLCache handles maxsize enforcement, LRU eviction, serialization, and statistics on top of the backend.

    When no backend is provided, the registered default is used (see MemoryCacheBackend or RedisCacheBackend).

    The type parameter T represents the cached value type. Defaults to Any when unspecified (TTLCache()). Use TTLCache[bytes] for raw bytes or TTLCache[MyModel] with a serializer/deserializer pair for typed caching.

    Raises:

    Type Description
    ValueError

    If maxsize is negative or ttl is not positive.

    Initialize the cache.

    get async 

    get(
    key: Annotated[str, Doc("The cache key.")],
    default: Annotated[
        T | None,
        Doc(
            "Value to return if the key is missing or expired."
        ),
    ] = None,
) -> T | None

    Get a value by key.

    Returns the default if the key is missing or expired. A hit promotes the key in LRU order.

    set async 

    set(
    key: Annotated[str, Doc("The cache key.")],
    value: Annotated[
        T,
        Doc(
            "The value to store. Must be bytes or serializable."
        ),
    ],
    ttl: Annotated[
        float | None,
        Doc(
            "Per-entry TTL override in seconds. Uses the default TTL if None."
        ),
    ] = None,
) -> None

    Set a value with an optional per-entry TTL override.

    If the cache is full (maxsize > 0), evicts the least recently used entry before storing.

    Raises:

    Type Description
    ValueError

    If ttl is not positive.
    TypeError

    If value is not bytes and no serializer is set.

    delete async 

    delete(
    key: Annotated[str, Doc("The cache key to delete.")],
) -> None

    Delete a key from the cache.

    No-op if the key does not exist.

    clear async 

    clear() -> None

    Remove all entries from the cache.

    cache_info 

    cache_info() -> CacheInfo

    Return a snapshot of cache statistics.

    cached

    Cached Decorator.

    cached 

    cached(
    cache: Annotated[
        TTLCache,
        Doc(
            "\n            The TTLCache instance to store results in.\n            "
        ),
    ],
    *,
    key_maker: Annotated[
        Callable[
            [
                Callable[..., Any],
                tuple[Any, ...],
                dict[str, Any],
            ],
            str,
        ]
        | None,
        Doc(
            "\n            Optional custom key generation function. Receives\n            ``(func, args, kwargs)`` and must return a string key.\n            "
        ),
    ] = None,
    skip: Annotated[
        Callable[[Any], bool] | None,
        Doc(
            "\n            Optional predicate receiving the function result. When\n            it returns ``True`` the result is **not** cached.\n            "
        ),
    ] = None,
    typed: Annotated[
        bool,
        Doc(
            "\n            If ``True``, arguments of different types are cached\n            separately (e.g. ``3`` vs ``3.0``).\n            "
        ),
    ] = False,
    lock: Annotated[
        _LockType,
        Doc(
            "\n            Enable stampede protection. When a cache miss occurs,\n            only one caller executes the function while all others\n            block until the result is available.\n\n            Set to ``True`` to enable with **per-key** locking:\n            concurrent misses on different keys proceed in parallel,\n            only callers hitting the same key are serialized.\n            The appropriate lock type is auto-created\n            (``asyncio.Lock`` for async, ``threading.Lock`` for\n            sync).\n\n            You can also pass a custom context manager instance\n            for **global** locking (a single lock shared across\n            all keys).\n            "
        ),
    ] = None,
) -> Callable[[Callable[P, R]], Callable[P, R]]

    Cache decorator for sync and async functions.

    Automatically detects whether the decorated function is sync or async and wraps it accordingly.

    The decorated function exposes cache_info() and cache_clear() helpers (matching functools.lru_cache). cache_clear() is always a coroutine (must be awaited).

    Returns:

    Type Description
    Callable[[Callable[P, R]], Callable[P, R]]

    A decorator that caches function results.

    Memory Cache Backend.

    MemoryCacheBackend 

    MemoryCacheBackend(
    *,
    auto_register: Annotated[
        bool,
        Doc(
            "Automatically register this cache backend in the backend registry."
        ),
    ] = True,
)

    In-memory cache backend.

    Stores entries in a Python dict with lazy TTL expiry. Suitable for testing and single-process applications.

    Initialize the memory cache backend.

    __aenter__ async 

    __aenter__() -> Self

    Open the cache backend.

    __aexit__ async 

    __aexit__(
    exc_type: type[BaseException] | None,
    exc_value: BaseException | None,
    traceback: TracebackType | None,
) -> None

    Close the cache backend.

    get async 

    get(*, key: str) -> bytes | None

    Get raw bytes by key.

    Returns None if the key is missing or expired. Expired entries are removed lazily on access.

    set async 

    set(*, key: str, value: bytes, ttl: float) -> None

    Store raw bytes with a TTL in seconds.

    delete async 

    delete(*, key: str) -> None

    Delete a key (no-op if absent).

    clear async 

    clear() -> None

    Remove all entries.

    Redis Cache Backend.

    RedisCacheBackend 

    RedisCacheBackend(
    url: Annotated[
        RedisDsn | str | None,
        Doc(
            "\n                The Redis URL.\n\n                If not provided, the URL will be taken from the\n                environment variables REDIS_URL or REDIS_HOST,\n                REDIS_PORT, REDIS_DB, and REDIS_PASSWORD.\n                "
        ),
    ] = None,
    *,
    prefix: Annotated[
        str,
        Doc(
            "\n                Prefix prepended to all Redis keys to avoid\n                conflicts with other keys.\n\n                By default no prefix is added.\n                "
        ),
    ] = "",
    auto_register: Annotated[
        bool,
        Doc(
            "Automatically register this cache backend in the backend registry."
        ),
    ] = True,
)

    Redis cache storage backend.

    Pure key-value storage with per-entry TTL handled natively by Redis (SETEX). Keys are prefixed for isolation.

    Must be used as an async context manager to manage the connection lifecycle.

    Initialize the Redis cache backend.

    __aenter__ async 

    __aenter__() -> Self

    Open the cache connection.

    __aexit__ async 

    __aexit__(
    exc_type: type[BaseException] | None,
    exc_value: BaseException | None,
    traceback: TracebackType | None,
) -> None

    Close the cache connection.

    get async 

    get(*, key: str) -> bytes | None

    Get raw bytes by key.

    Returns None if the key is missing or expired.

    set async 

    set(*, key: str, value: bytes, ttl: float) -> None

    Store raw bytes with a TTL in seconds.

    delete async 

    delete(*, key: str) -> None

    Delete a key (no-op if absent).

    clear async 

    clear() -> None

    Remove all entries matching the configured prefix.

    Uses SCAN to iterate keys without blocking Redis, then deletes in batches.

    Made with Material for MkDocs
